title: Stelis resupinata
taxonomy: Stelis resupinata is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family comprises approximately 30,000 species of orchids, which are one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. Stelis resupinata is also classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae, which is a large and widespread subfamily of orchids. Further classification places Stelis resupinata within the tribe Epidendreae, and the genus Stelis. The species is further classified within the subgenus Stelis.

intro: Stelis resupinata is a species of orchid native to tropical regions, characterized by its unique, flat growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Stelis resupinata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stelis resupinata is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture to treat respiratory conditions, and its extracts have also been used in traditional remedies for fever and digestive issues.
